title: Homalium ciliolatum
taxonomy: Homalium ciliolatum belongs to the family Salicaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as willows and their relatives. It is a genus of plants in the family Salicaceae, native to tropical Africa. The family Salicaceae is a part of the larger clade called Rosids within the angiosperm division of flowering plants. This classification places Homalium ciliolatum within the broader category of dicotyledonous plants. Further classification also groups Homalium ciliolatum under the order Malpighiales.

intro: Homalium ciliolatum is a species of plant in the family Salicaceae, commonly known as a type of willow or wild rose. The health benefits of Homalium ciliolatum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Homalium ciliolatum is often used in traditional herbal medicine and can be found in various preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which are commonly used to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
